title: Water Resource Specialists
description: Design or implement programs and strategies related to water resource issues such as supply, quality, and regulatory compliance issues.

tasks.text: Perform hydrologic, hydraulic, or water quality modeling.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Analyze storm water systems to identify opportunities for water resource improvements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Conduct, or oversee the conduct of, investigations on matters such as water storage, wastewater discharge, pollutants, permits, or other compliance and regulatory issues.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop strategies for watershed operations to meet water supply and conservation goals or to ensure regulatory compliance with clean water laws or regulations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct technical studies for water resources on topics such as pollutants and water treatment options.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Review or evaluate designs for water detention facilities, storm drains, flood control facilities, or other hydraulic structures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Present water resource proposals to government, public interest groups, or community groups.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop plans to protect watershed health or rehabilitate watersheds.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Write proposals, project reports, informational brochures, or other documents on wastewater purification, water supply and demand, or other water resource subjects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct cost-benefit studies for watershed improvement projects or water management alternatives.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide technical expertise to assist communities in the development or implementation of storm water monitoring or other water programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Compile and maintain documentation on the health of a body of water.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Identify and characterize specific causes or sources of water pollution.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct, or oversee the conduct of, chemical, physical, and biological water quality monitoring or sampling to ensure compliance with water quality standards.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Compile water resource data, using geographic information systems (GIS) or global position systems (GPS) software.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Recommend new or revised policies, procedures, or regulations to support water resource or conservation goals.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop or implement standardized water monitoring and assessment methods.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise teams of workers who capture water from wells and rivers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Negotiate for water rights with communities or water facilities to meet water supply demands.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor water use, demand, or quality in a particular geographic area.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Identify methods for distributing purified wastewater into rivers, streams, or oceans.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
